_comment: Pluto airband channel plan + front-end config (the OPERATIONAL source of truth). Copy this file to the ROOT of the Pluto's SD card as 'airband.json' (the card must be FAT32 -- the kernel has no exFAT). On boot the init script mounts the card at /mnt/sdcard and maia-httpd loads it via --airband-config /mnt/sdcard/airband.json; the web config UI (/airband.html) reads and writes THIS file. If the card is missing/unformatted/unmounted, maia-httpd falls back to its built-in default (a single channel, 118.050 AWOS, at 0 dB) -- hearing only a faint, single AWOS channel is the obvious cue that this SD plan did not load. samp_rate MUST be the value the channelizer was built for (audio rate = samp_rate / 128 / 7 = 15625 sps at 14 MHz). channels_hz: up to 21 absolute channel centers, all within center_hz +/- samp_rate/2. channel_labels: optional, parallel to channels_hz, cosmetic (web UI only). gain_db: fixed manual gain; 12 dB is tuned for an external LNA ahead of the Pluto. The receiver is INTERNAL-noise-limited (ch11 audio floor identical with antenna or 50 ohm load, within 0.4 dB; rises ~1 dB per +6 dB of gain), so the floor is ADC quantization / conducted comb downstream of the gain. At 0 dB the wanted signal sits AT that floor (audio SNR ~1 dB on the continuous 118.050 AWOS carrier; ~10-12 dB by 6-12 dB, plateauing to 42 dB); ~12 dB is the minimum that lifts voice clear of quantization without clipping the wideband ADC (0 %, ~7 dB headroom with the LNA). On a BARE front end (no external LNA) raise gain_db toward the 48 dB clipping knee. See firmware/diagnostics/ and SPUR-INVESTIGATION.md.
